Off Calendar Jun-05-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

J. Noah Hagey, Esq., BRAUNHAGEY & BORDEN LLP, via Zoom, (415) 599-0211, for Defendant.

Bryan Heckenlively, Esq., MUNGER, TOLLES & OLSON LLP, in court, (415) 512-4015, for Plaintiff.

Plaintiff STOCKBRIDGE TREASURE ISLAND INVESTMENT COMPANY, WMS TREASURE ISLAND DEVELOPMENT I, LLC's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint.

Ruling - A R G U E D. Upon conclusion of argument, the court adopts the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Stockbridge's motion to amend is granted. The motion is timely, there is a liberal policy in favor of amendment, and the proposed amendment does not prejudice Kenwood since there is no trial date and the amendment does not change the tenor of the case. Kenwood's merits arguments are not a reason to deny amendment. The proposed claim is not clearly barred and Kenwood raises factual issues. (Kittredge Sports Co. v. Superior Court (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048 ["[T]he preferable practice would be to permit the amendment and allow the parties to test its legal sufficiency"].)

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: R. Woods, Court Reporter: Teresa Kenworthy. CSR: 6673. T: (530) 308-2691. E: tkenworthy@gmail.com. (302/RBU)
